figures.caption: Figure 2: EAGLE training dynamics across 4 random seeds. (a) Training loss converges smoothly to ≈ 0.186 by epoch 40. (b) Validation AUC-ROC stabilises above 0.97 after 15 epochs. Stars mark the best-checkpoint epoch per seed.
Table 3: Feature audit. Temporal bounds and source for features are recorded to ensure that outcome information does not leak into the features.

Figure 1: EAGLE three-module architecture pipeline. (1) PatchTST-Lite: Each node’s 𝑇=14 time-step feature sequence is tokenised into 𝑁𝑝=2 non-overlapping patches of length 𝑝=7, encoded by a 2-layer Transformer (2 attention heads, 𝑑model=64), and mean-pooled to a fixed temporal embedding h𝑡 ∈ R64. (2) E-GAT: A 2-layer Edge-Aware GAT (4 heads per layer) aggregates neighbourhood information, conditioning attention coefficients jointly on node embeddings and static edge features E𝑢𝑣 (historical transit statistics, shipping mode distributions). (3) DualHead: Two independent MLP heads produce the SLA-violation probability 𝑝𝑣 ∈ 0, 1 (sigmoid) and expected delay magnitude 𝑑𝑣 ∈ R (Softplus), trained end-toend via a 0.7×BCE 0.3×Huber multi-task loss.
